content.problemStatement:
An integer xxx is said to be a **Perfect Power** if there exists **positive** integers aaa and bbb (i.e aaa, and bbb should be ‚â•1\geq 1‚â•1) such that x=ab+1x = a^{b+1}x=ab+1.

Given an integer nnn, find the closest Perfect Power to nnn. If there are multiple Perfect Powers equidistant from nnn and as close as possible to nnn, find the smallest one.

More formally, find the smallest integer xxx such that xxx is a Perfect Power and ‚à£n‚àíx‚à£‚â§‚à£n‚àíy‚à£|n - x| \leq |n - y|‚à£n‚àíx‚à£‚â§‚à£n‚àíy‚à£ over all Perfect Powers yyy.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer nnn.


Output Format
For each test case, print in a separate line, the smallest xxx such that xxx is a perfect power and ‚à£n‚àíx‚à£|n - x|‚à£n‚àíx‚à£ is minimum.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: n=7n = 7n=7, 8=238 = 2^38=23 is a perfect power, but 666 is not, so the minimum difference obtainable is 111 when x=8x = 8x=8.
Test case 222: n=10n = 10n=10, 9=329 = 3^29=32 is a perfect power, but 111111 is not, so the minimum difference obtainable is 111 when x=9x = 9x=9.
Test case 333: n=26n = 26n=26, 25=5225 = 5^225=52 is a perfect power, 27=3327 = 3^327=33 is also a perfect power. Both x=25x = 25x=25 and x=27x = 27x=27 give the minimum difference obtainable which is 111. The smallest of them is 252525.
Test case 444: n=242n = 242n=242, 243=35243 = 3^5243=35 is a perfect power but 241241241 is not, so the answer is 243243243.
Test case 555: 128=27128 = 2^7128=27 is closest to 129129129.
Test case 666: 394857628993920400=6283769802394857628993920400 = 628376980^2394857628993920400=6283769802 is the closest to 394857629456789876394857629456789876394857629456789876.
Test case 777: 353872815358410000=243904353872815358410000 = 24390^4353872815358410000=243904 is the closest to 353872815358409997353872815358409997353872815358409997.
